title: Moena Lisa

jokes.text: Homer struggling to remember something, muttering to his memory to wake up
jokes.setup: Homer clearly needs to remember something important but can't
jokes.punchline: His desperate internal dialogue with his own memory
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer bumbling - very character-specific and efficiently shows his mental process

jokes.text: Is it our anniversary? No, we don't have one this year.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to figure out what he's forgetting
jokes.punchline: The illogical idea that anniversaries are optional year-to-year
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Homer logic delivered with maximum economy

jokes.text: Don't you dare go limp. Too late. The deed is done. Kids, grab a fold.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he forgot something important
jokes.punchline: His dramatic physical collapse requiring the kids to carry him by grabbing his body folds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from emotional drama to grotesque physical comedy - 'grab a fold' is memorably disgusting

jokes.text: Moe's increasingly desperate voicemail messages about his birthday fishing trip
jokes.setup: Moe calling Homer about their fishing plans
jokes.punchline: His pathetic vulnerability - 'feeling kind of fragile' and 'listening for your car'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization - his desperate loneliness comes through authentically

jokes.text: I threw a javelin that barely missed Hitler. But I did hit an assassin who was trying to kill Hitler.
jokes.setup: Grampa talking about the 1936 Olympics
jokes.punchline: The absurd coincidence of accidentally saving Hitler by hitting his would-be assassin
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and historically absurd - the setup creates expectation of hitting Hitler, the twist is perfectly unexpected

jokes.text: The next time I saw Hitler, we had dinner and laughed about it.
jokes.setup: Following the javelin story
jokes.punchline: Grampa casually having dinner with Hitler like old friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurdity - treats Hitler like a casual acquaintance

jokes.text: What is this? Kill Hitler day?
jokes.setup: Grampa's rambling Hitler stories
jokes.punchline: Lisa's exasperated meta-commentary on the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction with maximum economy - captures audience's bewilderment

jokes.text: I collect Absolut ads. How many others do you have? There are others?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy offering himself as interview subject
jokes.punchline: His pathetic realization that his 'collection' might only be one ad
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - shows how delusional and pathetic his 'interests' are

jokes.text: Well, it's not fair when Moleman uses his replacement legs.
jokes.setup: Grampa losing at Senior Olympics events
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact complaint about prosthetic enhancement
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist beat - treats prosthetics as unfair athletic advantage

jokes.text: Oh, it's clouding up. I better put on me rain poncho.
jokes.setup: Track and field race starting
jokes.punchline: Grampa stopping mid-race to worry about weather
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa priorities - completely missing the point of the situation

jokes.text: Sir! You dropped your munchers!
jokes.setup: Grampa's dentures falling out during race
jokes.punchline: Official's polite term 'munchers' for dentures
jokes.explanation: Cute euphemism for dentures - mild wordplay with physical comedy

jokes.text: You'll never take me alive, Grim Reaper
jokes.setup: Grampa running in Senior Olympics
jokes.punchline: He thinks he's running from death itself
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Grampa's melodramatic relationship with mortality

jokes.text: Take the boy. His soul is fresher.
jokes.setup: Grampa running from 'death'
jokes.punchline: Immediately offering up Bart as substitute
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and selfish - perfect economy and character integration

jokes.text: I've never been happier.
jokes.setup: Grampa offering Bart to the Grim Reaper
jokes.punchline: Bart's cheerful acceptance of being sacrificed
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy callback - Bart's disturbing contentment with death

jokes.text: I wonder if the dog thought about us while we were gone.
jokes.setup: Family returning home from Senior Olympics
jokes.punchline: Cut to Santa's Little Helper clearly not thinking about them at all
jokes.explanation: Standard pet joke setup - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Everybody hide! Finally, that jerk Homer is home. Oh, it's just his car.
jokes.setup: Moe waiting for Homer all day
jokes.punchline: His bitter disappointment that it's only the car arriving
jokes.explanation: Good Moe characterization - his conflicted feelings about Homer

jokes.text: Happy birthday to me, I feel so damn lonely, Won't someone kill me... And many more.
jokes.setup: Moe singing birthday song to himself
jokes.punchline: Darkly suicidal lyrics ending with traditional 'and many more'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - subverts familiar song format with perfect character integration

jokes.text: Happy birthday to me / I feel so damn lonely / Won't someone kill me... / And many more.
jokes.setup: Moe singing happy birthday to himself
jokes.punchline: The increasingly dark lyrics ending with the traditional 'and many more'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the juxtaposition of suicidal lyrics with the cheerful birthday song format

jokes.text: Homer, we can't scuttle like crabs for the rest of our lives. Says you.
jokes.setup: Family hiding from Moe by crawling on floor
jokes.punchline: Homer's commitment to crab-scuttling lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist beat - Homer's stubborn commitment to avoiding responsibility

jokes.text: Dear Pus Bag? Whoa, Marge, who'd you piss off? It's for you, Pus Bag!
jokes.setup: Homer sees insulting letter salutation
jokes.punchline: Assuming it's for Marge, then learning it's actually for him
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection - Homer's assumption backfires perfectly

jokes.text: But you shattered my dreams like a Duff tall boy in a bar brawl, and you jammed the jagged neck right up my heart's butt.
jokes.setup: Moe's angry letter to Homer
jokes.punchline: Mixing poetic metaphor with crude bar imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe voice - attempting poetry but defaulting to crude bar metaphors

jokes.text: What a deep, tormented soul.
jokes.setup: Moe's overwrought angry letter
jokes.punchline: Lisa genuinely seeing artistic merit in his crude rant
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Lisa's intellectual pretension finding depth where there might be none

jokes.text: Loser!
jokes.setup: Emotional moment between Lisa and Moe
jokes.punchline: Nelson's drive-by insult
jokes.explanation: Standard Nelson beat - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Still in the top three. Still in the top ten.
jokes.setup: Moe calling this his best birthday ever
jokes.punchline: Rapidly downgrading his enthusiasm as he thinks about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating deflation - shows how pathetic his life really is

jokes.text: I ain't no great man, like Bob Seger or Haystacks Calhoun.
jokes.setup: Moe questioning why Lisa wants to interview him
jokes.punchline: His bizarre examples of 'great men'
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Moe's working-class heroes reveal his worldview

jokes.text: Moe, you live in a hotel? You're just like Eloise! Did someone say my name?
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing Moe to children's book character Eloise
jokes.punchline: Actual woman named Eloise appearing in the seedy hotel
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection - contrasts innocent children's reference with seedy reality

jokes.text: This vibrating massage chair feels great! That ain't a massage chair. It's just full of cockroaches. See?
jokes.setup: Bart enjoying what he thinks is a massage chair
jokes.punchline: It's actually just cockroaches making it vibrate
jokes.explanation: Gross-out visual gag with good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: I was in the Christmas pageant once. Mom showed up with a new boyfriend. He called me Steve.
jokes.setup: Moe's poetry fragments being read
jokes.punchline: Childhood trauma delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - childhood abandonment delivered with devastating simplicity

jokes.text: Ooh, Moe's a poet! He has a soul! Flutter your wings! It's tea time in Buttercup Junction!
jokes.setup: Learning Moe writes poetry
jokes.punchline: Barney's exaggerated feminine mockery
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction - Barney's crude masculinity threatened by poetry

jokes.text: Ooh, you love a man!
jokes.setup: Moe expressing gratitude to Homer
jokes.punchline: Barney's juvenile homophobic teasing
jokes.explanation: Dated humor that relies on homophobic anxiety - functional for character but crude

jokes.text: What-which why-liot's the who-now?
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning T.S. Eliot
jokes.punchline: Moe's confused word-scrambling of the name
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Moe's cultural ignorance through verbal confusion

jokes.text: It jumps out at you like a rat in your underwear drawer.
jokes.setup: Moe describing his poem title
jokes.punchline: His crude but vivid metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Moe characterization - his metaphors come from his seedy world

jokes.text: There once was a rapping tomato, That's right, I said 'rapping tomato', He rapped all day from April to May, And also, guess what, it was me.
jokes.setup: Homer's rejected poem being recited
jokes.punchline: The completely nonsensical limerick that ends with revealing he was the tomato
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly stupid Homer poetry - the meta-commentary and absurd twist work well

jokes.text: Stop the presses! Send my wife some flowers! And bring me an Advil! What do you mean you don't work for me?! You're hired! Now that you're hired, you're fired! Now that you don't work here, we can be friends. Now that we're friends, how come you never call? Some friend you are! God, I love this business.
jokes.setup: Editor excited about Moe's poem
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire manic monologue showing publishing world insanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating madness - captures publishing world stereotypes perfectly

jokes.text: Bad news, Maya Angelou: you've been bumped. ...you can't bump me... ...bump you!
jokes.setup: Moe's poem being featured on cover
jokes.punchline: Bumping famous poet Maya Angelou, who protests briefly
jokes.explanation: Solid celebrity cameo gag - Maya Angelou's brief protest is amusing

jokes.text: Screw you, snail trail! Who was that? Eh, some jerk making a prank call with a gag name: 'Tom Wolfe.'
jokes.setup: Moe getting call from famous author
jokes.punchline: Not recognizing Tom Wolfe as real, thinking it's a prank name
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Moe's ignorance causing him to miss major opportunity

jokes.text: Homer, don't drink and drive! Fine. I'll drive between sips.
jokes.setup: Marge warning Homer about drinking and driving
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation creating worse solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - finding the most dangerous interpretation of safety advice

jokes.text: Including Rhode Island! We're a small state, but we give big tickets.
jokes.setup: Meeting state troopers from across New England
jokes.punchline: Rhode Island's pride in their disproportionately harsh ticketing
jokes.explanation: Good regional humor - the small state compensating with harsh penalties is a believable character trait

jokes.text: He stands, stoop-shouldered, blinking in the light, hollow-chested like a dough-faced fall guy who's made a career of taking dives but has decided to get his manhood out of hock and take a shot at the title.
jokes.setup: Tom Wolfe meeting Moe
jokes.punchline: His elaborate, overwrought description of ordinary Moe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Wolfe parody - his verbose, dramatic writing style applied to mundane Moe

jokes.text: You ever scrub your hands real fast, and you think you're seeing a third one? One time I looked down and there really was. I gotta get a new lock for that bathroom.
jokes.setup: Moe responding to Tom Wolfe's flowery description
jokes.punchline: His disturbing bathroom story revealing seedy life details
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast to Wolfe's pretension - Moe's crude reality check with disturbing implications

jokes.text: 1876 was the price I paid for gas once. I thought of Burr when I saw it written on an Eskimo Pie package.
jokes.setup: Authors discussing needing help with titles
jokes.punchline: Gore Vidal's ridiculous explanations for his famous book titles
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of literary pretension - famous titles from mundane sources

jokes.text: Whoa, clear water. Swanky! Woo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Moe at the fancy literary festival
jokes.punchline: His amazement at basic amenities like clean water
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character work - his low standards revealed through excitement about basic water quality

jokes.text: Well, I dunno, uh... the only book I ever read was Super Hounds: The Ultimate Greyhound Betting System. So, uh, any of you wetnaps write that?
jokes.setup: Panel asking about Moe's literary influences
jokes.punchline: His only book being about dog track betting, calling famous authors 'wetnaps'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - his working-class reality clashing with literary world

jokes.text: Man, couples in this state sure like to clink glasses.
jokes.setup: Homer observing Vermont culture
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting gay marriage celebration as casual toasting
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor - Homer's innocent misunderstanding of what he's seeing

jokes.text: That was my house, you moron. Oh, I'm sorry. We should exchange insurance information. I have none.
jokes.setup: Homer driving through 'covered bridge' that's actually someone's house
jokes.punchline: The resident's poverty revealed through lack of insurance
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with mild social commentary on rural poverty

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Hey, st... stupid geese, you're eating my brilliance. Hey, hey, I will break your beak.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to write poem outdoors
jokes.punchline: Getting into literal fight with geese over his papers
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Moe's pretensions literally being eaten by nature

jokes.text: Yeah, go on, you cowards. Fly off in a 'V'.
jokes.setup: Moe fighting with geese
jokes.punchline: His specific insult about their flight formation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist insult - using geese's natural behavior as an insult is perfectly Moe

jokes.text: Oh, honey, don't cry. They're not made from real bears just their fur and noses.
jokes.setup: Lisa crying, Homer trying to comfort her about teddy bears
jokes.punchline: His 'comforting' explanation actually makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - trying to help but making things worse with disturbing details

jokes.text: Elevator capacity: 2,400 pounds. Inspection Certificate available in hotel manager's office. You just pulled that off the wall of the elevator.
jokes.setup: Moe presenting a keynote poem
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he's literally reading elevator inspection information as poetry
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Moe's desperate plagiarism exposed in the most mundane way possible

jokes.text: Ode to Lisa: Pointy-headed mini-muse, More friend than I deserved, You pulled me from the dirt, And made me the beautiful flower that I am.
jokes.setup: Moe finally delivering heartfelt poem
jokes.punchline: Mixing genuine sentiment with crude Moe-isms like 'pointy-headed'
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment - genuine sentiment filtered through Moe's crude perspective

jokes.text: Except for those two dudes I saw getting hitched. Not my thing, but I wish 'em well.
jokes.setup: Moe saying Lisa would make him happiest man in Vermont
jokes.punchline: His crude but tolerant observation about gay marriage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Moe's unexpected tolerance mixed with typical crudeness

jokes.text: Except for those two dudes I saw gettin' hitched. Not my thing, but I wish 'em well.
jokes.setup: Moe saying Lisa would make him the happiest man in Vermont
jokes.punchline: His casual aside about gay marriage with unexpectedly tolerant attitude
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Moe's unexpected tolerance and decency beneath his crude exterior

jokes.text: Ooh, you fight like Anne Rice.
jokes.setup: Literary food fight escalating
jokes.punchline: Using another author's name as a fighting insult
jokes.explanation: Decent literary insult but not particularly clever or original

jokes.text: I need photos. Photos of Spider-Man. This is a poetry journal. Okay, then poems about Spider-Man.
jokes.setup: Poetry journal editor giving assignments
jokes.punchline: J. Jonah Jameson character demanding Spider-Man content in wrong medium
jokes.explanation: Good crossover gag - J. Jonah Jameson's obsession applied to wrong context

tmdb_overview: Lisa sees poetry in the notes Moe sticks to the walls of his hotel room, so she sends his work to a poetry journal, which publishes it, making Moe the toast of the literary circuit.
